title: acer saccharophorum var. nigrum
intro: Acer saccharophorum var. nigrum, also known as black sugar maple, is a lesser-known variety of the sugar maple tree, specifically a black-stemmed version native to parts of Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which help to treat wounds, skin irritations, and digestive issues. The health benefits of Acer saccharophorum var. nigrum include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate coughs, and soothe digestive problems, making it a valuable herbal remedy for various ailments. Herbal preparations of the plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be prepared by steeping the leaves and bark in hot water or a solvent like ethanol.
